title: Exercise/solution framework + autograding scaffolding
context: Each teaching dataset ships with exercises + worked solutions, but without a structured framework and autograder, there is no CI check that solutions are correct and exercises are well-formed. This task creates the exercise schema (integrated with the canonical metadata model's exercises[] block from template-001) and an autograding scaffold that can be run in CI: a reference solution scores full marks, a known-wrong answer does not, and both are deterministic. This makes 'the teaching layer is correct' a checkable assertion rather than a manual review concern. Depends on template-001 (exercise schema integration) and tooling-006 (CI harness).
objective: Implement and merge an exercise/solution framework with autograding scaffolding that integrates with the canonical metadata model and runs deterministically in CI, validating that reference solutions score full marks and wrong answers do not.
acceptanceCriteria: Exercise schema (prompt, difficulty, solutionRef, expectedOutput or evaluation function) integrated with the canonical metadata model's exercises[] block from template-001.
Autograder runs in CI: a committed reference solution scores full marks and a known-wrong answer does not; fully deterministic.
Framework supports at least one exercise type relevant to the pilot dataset (e.g. code-output comparison for survival analysis).
Code MIT licensed; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; no credentials committed; commit DCO signed-off.
Educator reviewer has confirmed the framework is usable for the teaching-layer tasks (ds-expr-lesson-014 and future lessons).
resources: PLAN.md (§Scope — Light tooling; §Quality — Test fixtures & golden files)
output: A merged PR adding the exercise/solution framework and autograding scaffolding, with CI demonstrating reference solution = full marks and wrong answer = fail.
